text: A resistor of resistance \( R \) is connected between the terminals of a cell of emf \( E \) and internal resistance \( r \). If \( I \) is the current through the circuit, the terminal potential difference of the cell is given by
explanation:
When the cell is discharging, the terminal potential difference across the cell is given by

\( V = E - Ir \)
where \( I \) is the current drawn by the cell.
name: Questions Item 1

text: In a potentiometer experiment the balancing length with a cell is 560 cm. When an external resistance of 10 Ω is connected in parallel to the cell, then the balancing length changes by 60 cm. Find the internal resistance of the cell.
explanation:
Given \( l_1 = 560 \) cm and \( l_2 = 500 \) cm.

Internal resistance of cell is given by

\( r = \left( \frac{l_1 - l_2}{l_2} \right) R = \left( \frac{560 - 500}{500} \right) \times 10 = 1.2 \) Ω.
name: Questions Item 2

text: In a balanced meter bridge, the segment of wire opposite to a known resistance of 70 Ω is 70 cm. The unknown resistance is
explanation:
In balanced meter bridge known resistance \( S = 70 \) Ω and \( l = 70 \) cm.

Unknown resistance,

\( R = S \left( \frac{100 - l}{l} \right) = 70 \left( \frac{100 - 70}{70} \right) = 30 \) Ω.
name: Questions Item 3

text: Resistance of a tungsten wire at 150°C is 133 Ω. Its temperature coefficient of resistance is 0.0045 °C⁻¹. The resistance of this wire at 500°C is
name: Questions Item 4

text: The V-I graph for a conductor at temperature \( T_1 \) and \( T_2 \) are as shown. \( T_2 - T_1 \) is proportional to
explanation:
Resistance \( R_1 \propto T_1 \Rightarrow \tan \theta = KT_1 \)

\( R_2 \propto T_2 \Rightarrow \cot \theta = KT_2 \)

\( T_2 - T_1 = \frac{\cos 2\theta}{K \sin 2\theta} \Rightarrow T_2 - T_1 \propto \cot 2\theta \).
name: Questions Item 5

text: A copper wire of radius 0.1 mm and resistance 2 kΩ is connected across a power supply of 40 V. The number of electrons transferred per second between the supply and the wire at one end is
explanation:
Current \( I = \frac{40}{2 \times 10^3} = 2 \times 10^{-2} \) A.

Charge per second \( q = 2 \times 10^{-2} \) C.

Number of electrons \( n = \frac{q}{e} = \frac{2 \times 10^{-2}}{1.6 \times 10^{-19}} = 1.25 \times 10^{17} \).
name: Questions Item 6

text: The wire of potentiometer has resistance 4 Ω and length 1 m. It is connected to a cell of emf 2 V and internal resistance 1 Ω. The current flowing through the potentiometer wire is
explanation: Current \( I = \frac{E}{R + r} = \frac{2}{4 + 1} = 0.4 \) A.
name: Questions Item 7

text: In given figure, an ammeter reads 5 A and voltmeter reads 40 V. The actual value of resistance \( R \) is
explanation:
Voltmeter has very high resistance, so current through it is nearly zero.

\( I_1 \approx 5 \) A

\( \frac{40}{R} < 5 \Rightarrow R > 8 \) Ω.
name: Questions Item 8

text: The external diameter of 5 m long hollow tube is 0.1 m and thickness of its wall is 0.005 m. If \( \rho = 1.7 \times 10^{-8} \) Ω-m, then its resistance will be
name: Questions Item 9

text: Potential difference between the points P and Q in the circuit shown is
explanation:
Equivalent resistance \( R_{PQ} = \frac{48}{25} \) Ω.

\( V_{PQ} = I \cdot R_{PQ} = 1.5 \times \frac{48}{25} = 2.88 \) V.
name: Questions Item 10

text: Two wires of equal diameters, lengths \( l_1, l_2 \) and having resistivities \( S_1, S_2 \) respectively are joined in series. The equivalent resistivity of the combination is
name: Questions Item 11

text: The colour code for a resistance of 22 Ω ± 5% is
name: Questions Item 12

text: Four resistors A, B, C and D form a Wheatstone bridge. The bridge is balanced when C = 100 Ω. If A and B are interchanged, the bridge balances for C = 121 Ω. The value of D is
name: Questions Item 13

text: In the circuit shown, if the current through the resistor R is 1/5 A, the value of R is
explanation:
Equivalent emf \( e = \frac{1}{3} \) V and internal resistance \( r = \frac{2}{3} \) Ω.

\( \frac{1}{5} = \frac{1/3}{2/3 + R} \Rightarrow R = 1 \) Ω.
name: Questions Item 14

text:
Assertion (A): When a wire of aluminium and another wire of silicon are heated from room temperature to 80°C, the conductivity of aluminium increases and that of silicon decreases.
Reason (R): Aluminium has positive temperature coefficient of resistivity and silicon has negative temperature coefficient of resistivity.
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct.
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct.
explanation: Aluminium is metal (positive temperature coefficient), conductivity decreases. Silicon is semiconductor (negative temperature coefficient), conductivity increases. Hence (A) is incorrect but (R) is correct.
name: Questions Item 15

text: The walls of a closed cubical box of edge 60 cm are made of material of thickness 1 mm and thermal conductivity 4 × 10^{-4} cal s^{-1} cm^{-1} °C^{-1}. The interior of the box is maintained 1000°C above the outside temperature by a heater placed inside the box and connected across 400 V DC supply. The resistance of the heater is
explanation:
Heat transfer rate \( P = 361.49 \) W.

\( R = \frac{V^2}{P} = \frac{400^2}{361.49} \approx 0.4426 \) Ω.
name: Questions Item 16

text: In the given circuit, the electric currents through 15 Ω and 6 Ω respectively are
explanation: Applying KVL, \( I_1 = 0.5 \) A and \( I_2 = 0 \) A.
name: Questions Item 17

text: In the given circuit, the current through 2 Ω resistor is
explanation:
At steady state, capacitor acts as open circuit.

Applying KVL, \( I_2 = 0.9 \) A.
name: Questions Item 18

text: The length of a potentiometer wire is \( l \). A cell of emf \( E \) is balanced at a length \( \frac{l}{3} \) from positive end of the wire. If the length of the wire is increased by \( \frac{l}{2} \), the distance at which the same cell gives the balancing point is
explanation: New balancing length \( l' = \frac{l}{2} \).
name: Questions Item 19

text: \( n \) identical resistances are taken in which \( \frac{n}{2} \) resistors are joined in series in the left gap and the remaining \( \frac{n}{2} \) resistances are joined in parallel in the right gap of a metre bridge. Balancing length in cm is
explanation: Balancing length \( l = \frac{100 n^2}{n^2 + 4} \) cm.
name: Questions Item 20

text: Electrical energy costs 25 paisa per kilowatt hour. Assuming that no energy is wasted, the cost of heating 4.6 kg of water from 25°C to the boiling point is
explanation:
Heat required \( Q = 1449 \times 10^3 \) J.

Units \( N = 0.40 \).

Cost = 10 paisa.
name: Questions Item 21

text: A 500 W heater is designed to operate at 200 V potential difference. If it is connected across 160 V line, the heat it will produce in 20 minutes is
explanation:
Resistance \( R = 80 \) Ω.

New power \( P_2 = 320 \) W.

Heat \( = 384 \) kJ.
name: Questions Item 22

text: A cell of emf \( e \) and internal resistance \( r \) is connected across a variable load resistance \( R \). The graph drawn between its terminal voltage and resistance \( R \) is
explanation: Terminal voltage \( V = \frac{E R}{R + r} \). Graph starts from origin and increases.
name: Questions Item 23

text: In a meter-bridge if the left and right gaps are connected with 2 Ω and 3 Ω resistances, respectively then the bridge is balanced. The resistance to be connected with 3 Ω resistance to get the balancing point at midpoint of the bridge wire is
explanation: For midpoint balance, \( \frac{2}{3 \parallel x} = 1 \Rightarrow x = 6 \) Ω in parallel.
name: Questions Item 24

text: The equivalent resistance between A and B is 6 Ω. The value of \( R_1 \) is
explanation:
After short-circuiting 2 Ω and 3 Ω,

\( \frac{15 R_1}{15 + R_1} = 6 \Rightarrow R_1 = 10 \) Ω.
name: Questions Item 25
